title: Create mutes table migration and GET /api/v1/mutes endpoint
description:
## What
Create `mutes` table with columns `(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, muter_persona_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES personas(id), muted_account_id INTEGER NOT NULL, notifications BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE, expires_at INTEGER, created_at INTEGER NOT NULL, UNIQUE(muter_persona_id, muted_account_id))`. Implement GET /api/v1/mutes returning paginated list of muted Account objects with Link header pagination.

## Why
Without the mutes table and list endpoint, no mute functionality can be built; clients need the list to show users who they have muted.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social /api/v1/mutes and document JSON shape (array of Account objects), Link header pagination format, and what fields appear on each muted Account entry
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: write the SQLite migration for the mutes table with proper foreign keys and unique constraint; implement GET /api/v1/mutes with cursor-based Link header pagination using snowflake IDs
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write tests that mute two accounts, call GET /api/v1/mutes, assert both accounts appear as Account objects in the response with correct shape

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social /api/v1/mutes returns array of Account objects (same shape as /api/v1/accounts/:id), with Link header for pagination; page size default 40
- Test approach: populate mutes table directly, call endpoint, assert response shape matches Account schema; test Link header is present when more results exist

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Foreign key constraint on muter_persona_id -- migration must include REFERENCES personas(id)
- UNIQUE constraint prevents duplicate mutes -- handle constraint violation gracefully (return existing mute, not 500)
- Pagination uses parameterized max_id/since_id -- no string interpolation into SQL
